judgments.rationale: All three alternatives are live and have a complete common comparison field, so scoring is possible. Bet-b, the selected bet, has materially adverse evidence from a direct client reply on accountability; this outweighs its modest score lead and places it behind alternatives with only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against them. Bet-a is strongest because it has high comparative scores without material sourced contradiction. Bet-c ranks above bet-b because it lacks the material direct-client evidence against the selected option, despite weaker continuity and implementation scores. The selected position cannot be High and is Low due to the material sourced evidence against it.

bet-b has a material, directly sourced client-reply objection on accountability. That adverse evidence dominates its small nominal score advantages over bet-a and makes the selected position less robust than the alternatives. bet-a has strong comparative scores with only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against; bet-c has lower continuity and implementation scores but likewise no material sourced contradiction. All three are live and have complete comparison fields, so no hard stop applies.

Selected bet: bet-b. bet-b has the strongest stated criterion scores, but its position is not robust because it alone carries material evidence against from a direct client reply, while bet-a is a close live alternative with only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against. That sourced material concern outweighs bet-b’s small score advantage. bet-c ranks last on the stated scores, despite no additional fog exposure.

Selected bet: bet-c. Although bet-c has the highest nominal weighted criterion score, it carries a critical, unaddressed fog dependency with very high influence (0.9). This materially unresolved critical exposure caps the selected position at Low and makes it less robust than the otherwise similarly evidenced live alternatives. Bet-a ranks first on the supplied score profile without fog exposure; bet-b ranks next, with stronger implementation but lower continuity and accountability than bet-a. All alternatives share only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against and partially tested limited failure modes.

Selected bet bet-c has the strongest scored comparison profile, but it carries a critical, unaddressed fog dependency with 0.9 influence. This materially destabilizes its position and prevents a rating above Low. Bet-a is most robust because it has no fog dependency and exceeds bet-b on the more heavily weighted continuity criterion; both otherwise share only weak generated evidence against and a limited partially tested failure mode. Bet-b is next. The common comparison field is complete and there are three credible live alternatives, so no hard-stop NoScore applies.

Selected bet: bet-c. It has the strongest stated criterion scores, followed closely by bet-a and then bet-b. All three are live, grounded, and comparably exposed to only weak generated-hypothesis evidence against plus partially tested limited failures. However, bet-c carries a critical, high-influence (0.9) unaddressed fog dependency, so its lead is not robust enough for High despite its score advantage. No hard stop applies and there are three credible live alternatives.

Selected bet-a has the strongest criterion scores, but its catastrophic failure mode is untested, creating critical unresolved failure exposure and capping confidence at Low. bet-b is most robust: it has no fog exposure, only the shared weak generated evidence against, and no catastrophic untested failure; it also outperforms bet-c on the more heavily weighted continuity and implementation criteria. bet-c ranks second, with weaker continuity and implementation despite slightly stronger accountability. The selected bet is bet-a.

bet-a is the selected bet but carries a catastrophic, untested failure mode. This is a critical unresolved failure exposure, so the selected position cannot exceed Low despite its stronger criterion scores. bet-b is most robust among the live alternatives because it has stronger continuity and implementation scores than bet-c, with the same weak generated evidence against and the same limited partially tested failure exposure. bet-c ranks next. All three alternatives are live, grounded, and have complete criterion fields; no hard stop applies.

Selected bet: bet-a. No hard stop applies and all three alternatives are live with populated common comparison fields. bet-b is most robust: it has the strongest implementation and materially stronger continuity than bet-c, without an untested catastrophic failure mode. bet-c is second: lower continuity and implementation than bet-b, but no critical untested failure is recorded. bet-a has the highest nominal criterion scores overall, but its catastrophic failure mode is untested; this critical unresolved failure exposure caps the selected position at Low and places it behind the alternatives without that exposure.

NoScore: the common comparison field is incomplete because hardStops.coverageResolved is false. Under the stated rule, this is a hard stop regardless of the otherwise live alternatives.
NoScore: coverageResolved is false, leaving the common comparison field incomplete. Under the stated rule, this is a hard stop regardless of the candidate scores or live-alternative count.
Hard stop: coverageResolved is false. The common comparison field is incomplete, so the live alternatives cannot be robustly ranked.
NoScore: the matrix contains only one live alternative (bet-a). Fewer than two credible live alternatives prevents a robustness ranking, regardless of the selected bet’s individual support.
NoScore: the matrix contains only one live alternative. Fewer than two credible live alternatives prevents a robustness comparison.
Hard stop: the matrix contains only one credible live alternative (bet-a). With fewer than two live alternatives, decision robustness cannot be ranked or scored.
